ops.title: Track F drain-consumer contract checklist
ops.body:
The drain package's crash-safety was verified over five adversarial rounds (2026-07-18). Three windows are closed by CONSUMER contracts, not mechanisms — the consumer wiring must satisfy and test each:

1. **Idempotent Yield (at-least-once).** A crash between a successful `Resources.Yield` and the durable terminalize replays the Yield on restart. fusekit's lease-fence yield must be idempotent (re-yielding an already-yielded lease is a no-op). Verify with a double-yield test against the real implementation.

2. **Exclusive fence blocks successor registrations.** While the fence for a key is held, the successor must be UNABLE to produce new canonical registrations for that key — the sweep's ownership revalidation re-reads canonical under the held fence and leans on this exclusivity; a non-exclusive fence reopens a double-apply (verdicts V1/V5, round 5). fusekit's mount lease must make successor activity on the key impossible while held. Verify at the p6 VM drain scenarios.

3. **Gated spawn wiring.** Every consumer `proc.Spawn` must set `Gate: store.SpawnGate()` (one StrikeStore shared by RunConfig and TransitionConfig paths). The contract is godoc-enforced, not type-enforced — an ungated Spawn silently escapes strike accounting.

Also new required wiring since the hardening rounds: `RunConfig.Canonical` (canonical journal for ownership-aware sweep), `AdoptDead(expected proc.Identity)` (pass the identity your scan proved dead), `NewGeneration` returns an error (single-segment allowlisted names only), `ErrSeqExhausted` from Register at seq saturation (fail-fast, not retryable), `ErrStaleGeneration` on incarnation mismatch.

Out-of-scope by design (package godoc): hostile same-UID actors staging symlinks in the dotdir — deferred with the XPC/per-message-identity wave.
ops.when: wiring fusekit (or any consumer) onto daemonkit drain — Track F of the extraction plan
